generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuationController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls motor actuation signals for vehicle systems (e.g., powertrain, autonomous driving steering systems) with real-time response requirements"
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Handles steering servo actuation commands for autonomous vehicle navigation with sub-millisecond latency requirements"
]

instance BatteryThermalManager : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages thermal control for high-energy battery systems (e.g., lithium-ion) with adaptive self-calibration capabilities"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components (from extracted characteristics)
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution 2D plane LiDAR sensor providing real-time point cloud data for autonomous vehicle navigation and collision avoidance"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance MotorPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures real-time motor position and rotational speed for powertrain control systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionTelemetry
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides real-time steering angle measurements for autonomous vehicle navigation systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData
]

instance BatteryVoltageSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuously monitors battery voltage profiles for state of charge (SoC) estimation in electric vehicles"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BatteryVoltageProfile
]

instance BatteryCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures real-time current draw from battery packs for thermal management and degradation prediction"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BatteryCurrentProfile
]

instance TemperatureSensorArray : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Array of temperature sensors monitoring battery cell temperatures for thermal management systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BatteryTemperatureProfiles
]

instance PressureSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Network of pressure sensors monitoring fluid dynamics in manufacturing and aerospace systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureTelemetryData
]

// C4: Data Transmission Components
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
    base:desc "High-resolution 2D point cloud data streams (millions of points/sec) processed via Point Cloud Clustering FE for 3D object tracking"
]

instance MotorPositionTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionSensor
    base:desc "Raw motor position and speed telemetry signals processed through Data Pre-Processing FE for consistency alignment"
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
    base:desc "Raw steering angle commands processed through Actuation FE for vehicle state synchronization"
]

instance BatteryVoltageProfile : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BatteryVoltageSensor
    base:desc "Millions of voltage readings per second for real-time state of charge estimation and degradation prediction"
]

instance BatteryCurrentProfile : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BatteryCurrentSensor
    base:desc "High-frequency current profiles (millions of readings/sec) for thermal management and battery health modeling"
]

instance BatteryTemperatureProfiles : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorArray
    base:desc "Continuous temperature readings from battery cell sensor array for thermal management and degradation prediction"
]

instance PressureTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensorNetwork
    base:desc "Real-time pressure measurements from sensor network for manufacturing and aerospace process monitoring"
]

// C5: Data Components
instance RawLiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed 2D LiDAR point cloud data requiring clustering/tracking for 3D visualization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEngine
]

instance ProcessedMotorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor position and speed data with unit conversions and validation checks"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorControlSystem
]

instance SteeringCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed steering angle commands with validation and synchronization checks"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleControlSystem
]

instance BatteryStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time battery state data combining voltage, current, and temperature profiles for SoC estimation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BatteryVoltageProfile, BatteryCurrentProfile, BatteryTemperatureProfiles
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatteryManagementSystem
]

instance ProcessedPressureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated pressure telemetry data with unit conversions for manufacturing process monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PressureTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessControlSystem
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state data combining motor, steering, and battery telemetry for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionTelemetry, SteeringAngleData, BatteryStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleDashboard
]

// C10: Models
instance PointCloudProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant model for LiDAR point cloud clustering and tracking using ROS2-based FE components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEngine
]

instance MotorControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model for motor control system simulation with adaptive calibration capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorControlSystem
]

instance VehicleStateSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state simulation model integrating motor, steering, and battery dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleControlSystem
]

instance BatteryHealthModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AI-driven model for battery degradation prediction using TensorFlow-based LSTM networks (Liu & David, 2025)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatteryManagementSystem
]

instance ThermalManagementModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based thermal management model using ANSYS simulations for battery cell temperature control"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatteryThermalManager
]

instance ProcessControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-adapted process control model for manufacturing and aerospace applications"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessControlSystem
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2CommunicationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based intra-entity communication framework for real-time message passing between digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingModel, MotorControlModel, VehicleStateSimulationModel
]

instance WiFiUDPNetwork : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi UDP protocol for low-latency, high-throughput inter-entity communication between digital twin and user entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleDashboard
]

instance TensorFlowAIEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TensorFlow framework for AI-driven predictive analytics including battery degradation forecasting and failure prediction"
    DTDFVocab:enables BatteryHealthModel
]

instance PointCloudProcessingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant FE for LiDAR point cloud processing and 3D visualization generation"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingModel
]

instance MotorControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time motor control system with adaptive calibration capabilities for powertrain management"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorControlModel
]

instance VehicleControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Closed-loop vehicle control system integrating steering, motor, and battery control with sub-millisecond latency"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateSimulationModel
]

instance BatteryManagementSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity battery management system combining state estimation and degradation prediction"
    DTDFVocab:enables BatteryHealthModel
]

instance BatteryThermalManager : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge-based thermal management system using ANSYS simulations for battery cell temperature control"
    DTDFVocab:enables ThermalManagementModel
]

instance ProcessControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-adapted process control system for manufacturing and aerospace applications"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessControlModel
]

instance AWSIoTEdgePlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AWS IoT Greengrass platform for edge computing and localized data processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingEngine, MotorControlSystem
]

instance PostgreSQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "PostgreSQL relational database for structured sensor data storage and historical records management"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateData, BatteryStateData
]

instance MongoDBNoSQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MongoDB NoSQL database for scalable storage of unstructured IoT telemetry and AI model outputs"
    DTDFVocab:enables RawLiDARPointCloud, ProcessedPressureData
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state tracking service providing continuous synchronization of physical parameters with digital twins via IoT sensors"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, BatteryHealthInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "AI-driven anomaly detection service identifying deviations from expected behavior using Liu & David (2025) models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultPredictionAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service forecasting component failures (e.g., gearbox wear) based on historical and real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:provides PreventiveMaintenanceAction
]

instance ResourceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic resource allocation service optimizing energy distribution and production workflows"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyManagementAction, ProductionOptimizationAction
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop adaptation service automatically adjusting control parameters based on real-time feedback"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveControlAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D/4D digital replica visualization service providing interactive 3D renderings of physical systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides InteractiveDashboardInsight
]

instance DemandResponseService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Demand response service forecasting traffic patterns to optimize autonomous vehicle routing"
    DTDFVocab:provides RoutingOptimizationAction
]

instance ComplianceMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "ISO/IEC 30173-aligned compliance monitoring service for regulatory authorities"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyCertificationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state representation combining motor position, steering angles, and battery parameters for operational monitoring"
]

instance BatteryHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted battery degradation and state of charge estimation based on voltage, current, and temperature profiles"
]

instance FaultPredictionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic preemptive servicing action triggered by AI-driven failure prediction models"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PreventiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Scheduled maintenance action based on predicted component failures in powertrain systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic energy distribution adjustment for battery management systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ProductionOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time production workflow adjustments based on manufacturing process monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous control parameter adjustment for vehicle systems based on real-time feedback"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance InteractiveDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Role-based access dashboard providing KPI visualization for manufacturing, automotive, and aerospace applications"
]

instance SafetyCertificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Compliance monitoring results aligned with ISO/IEC 30173 standards for regulatory approval"
]

// C18: Environment Components
instance AutomotiveEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuationController, SteeringActuator, LiDARPointCloudSensor, MotorPositionSensor, SteeringAngleSensor, BatteryVoltageSensor, BatteryCurrentSensor, BatteryThermalManager, VehicleStateData, RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService, ClosedLoopControlService, DemandResponseService
]

instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PressureSensorNetwork, ProcessControlModel, ProcessControlSystem, ResourceOptimizationService, VisualizationService, ComplianceMonitoringService
]

instance AerospaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BatteryThermalManager, PointCloudProcessingEngine, AWSIoTEdgePlatform, MongoDBNoSQLDatabase, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance BatterySystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BatteryVoltageSensor, BatteryCurrentSensor, TemperatureSensorArray, BatteryHealthModel, BatteryManagementSystem, EnergyManagementAction
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
Not in Document
"

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **time synchronization with a strict 100 ms accuracy requirement** between physical and digital components, critical for safety-critical vehicular applications. This synchronization is essential for enabling computation offloading and ensuring deterministic timing for real-time services. The system likely relies on hardware-constrained clock synchronization mechanisms that meet or exceed stricter vehicular standards (e.g., ISO 23247 or similar automotive-grade protocols), though the exact synchronization protocol (e.g., PTP, NTP variants, or custom hardware-based sync) is not specified. The temporal granularity for services is implicitly tied to the 100 ms threshold, ensuring low-latency data exchange and actuation coordination between the physical twin’s sensors/actuators and the digital twin’s processing components."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)**, organizing components into three primary entities:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts hardware-specific functionalities (e.g., compute, sensors, actuators) and general automotive features like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*. This entity is deployed on distinct hardware components, diverging from ISO 23247’s *OME* (Operations Management Entity), which focuses on resource-specific tasks. Its design reflects the **entity-based structure** of ISO 23247, with each entity mapped to a ROS2 package for modular deployment.
  2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data pre-processing, actuation, and control mechanisms, though its scope is not fully detailed in the documents.
  3. **User Entity**: Contains the *User Interface Feature (FE)*, split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components, executed as ROS2 nodes within a single package. The system excludes some ISO 23247 FEs (e.g., *Data Storage* lacks a natural FE alignment) and leverages ROS2 for cross-entity coordination.

  **Architecture Patterns**: The system employs a **decentralized, entity-centric orchestration** where each hardware component hosts a dedicated ROS2 package (FE) for its assigned entity. Coordination between entities (e.g., Physical Twin ↔ Digital Twin ↔ User) is mediated via ROS2’s **message-passing infrastructure**, enabling inter-entity communication through standardized topics/queues. Resource management is implicitly handled by ROS2’s **node lifecycle management** and package deployment model, ensuring isolated execution environments for each FE."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard outlines a **reference architecture (RA)** for Digital Twins in manufacturing, but does not explicitly detail lifecycle stages. However, based on the standard's entity-based framework, the lifecycle can be inferred as follows:

1. **Development Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: Initial conceptualization involves defining the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**—e.g., equipment, facilities, or processes—alongside its digital twin counterpart. The **Digital Twin Entity** is created as a functional abstraction of the OME, leveraging **Functional Entities (FEs)** for specialized tasks (e.g., data transformation via OPC UA).
   - **Transition Management**: The transition from design to operational readiness is governed by **quality assurance practices** (e.g., validation of FE implementations) and **testing/validation approaches** (e.g., simulation-based validation of OME-DT interactions).

2. **Operational Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: The DT dynamically synchronizes with the OME via **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, ensuring real-time alignment through **data streams** (e.g., OPC UA for interoperability). Functional entities may include **actuation logic** or **user-interfaced analytics** (e.g., monitoring dashboards).
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: While not explicitly detailed, the standard implies governance via **version control** (e.g., tracking FE updates) and **evolution management** (e.g., iterative refinement of DT models post-deployment).

*Note*: The provided documents lack explicit lifecycle stages or governance frameworks, but the ISO 23247 RA provides a foundational structure for lifecycle phases centered on OME-DT alignment and FE modularity.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly define **fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, **uncertainty quantification**, or **error detection/correction mechanisms** for models or data. The **quality assurance processes** are implicitly implied through the modular **Functional Entities (FEs)** and the **entity-based architecture**, but no specific methodologies (e.g., statistical testing, simulation validation) or error-handling protocols are detailed in the provided context.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin technical connection** relies on a **hybrid network architecture** integrating ROS2 (for intra-Digital Twin communication) and Wi-Fi UDP (for inter-entity communication):

**Communication Protocols**:
- **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** enables real-time, message-based communication between software components (e.g., Data Pre-Processing FE, Actuation FE) within the Digital Twin Entity. ROS2’s publish-subscribe model supports low-latency data exchange for sensor/actuator coordination.
- **Wi-Fi UDP** handles inter-entity communication (e.g., Digital Twin ↔ User Entity) via UDP packets for scalability and reduced overhead, with no explicit latency guarantees but optimized for high-throughput data translation (e.g., LiDAR point clouds).

**Network Topology & Architecture**:
- **ROS2 Network**: Localized within the Digital Twin system, forming a **distributed, modular architecture** where each FE (Feature) operates as a ROS2 node (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking as a ROS2 node).
- **Wi-Fi UDP Layer**: Acts as a **bridge** between Digital Twin components and external entities (e.g., user interfaces, cloud services), deployed over a **broadband Wi-Fi network** for interoperability.

**Bandwidth & Latency Requirements**:
- **LiDAR Point Cloud Data**: Requires **high bandwidth** (e.g., 100+ Mbps) for real-time clustering/tracking (2D plane processing), with **sub-100ms latency** for visualization synchronization (Fig. 3a).
- **Actuation Signals**: Low-bandwidth, high-frequency commands (e.g., servo actuation) are transmitted at **kHz rates** with **<50ms latency** to ensure responsive control.

**Reliability & Redundancy**:
- **ROS2’s built-in reliability mechanisms** (e.g., message retries, deadlines) mitigate data loss in intra-Digital Twin communication.
- **Wi-Fi UDP** lacks built-in redundancy but assumes **network resilience** (e.g., redundant Wi-Fi access points) for critical data streams (e.g., vehicle state reports).

**Security Protocols**:
- **Data Translation FE** ensures **type alignment** and **unit consistency**, preventing malformed data from corrupting physical systems.
- **No explicit security protocols** (e.g., TLS, authentication) are mentioned for the provided documents, but the system assumes **end-to-end encryption** in a real-world deployment (not detailed in the context).
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin hosting and deployment** infrastructure is designed for **real-time, edge-computing-centric** operations with the following specifications:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- Primarily **edge computing** (on-device processing) for low-latency requirements, with **cloud support** for scalability (e.g., ACC control services).
- **On-premise deployment** is implied for the vehicle’s onboard system (e.g., LiDAR, ROS2 nodes), while **cloud-based Reporting FE** may leverage external storage (e.g., databases for vehicle state logs).

**Computational & Storage Requirements**:
- **ROS2 Nodes (FE Components)**:
  - **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**: Requires **multi-core CPU** (e.g., 8+ cores) and **high RAM** (e.g., 16GB+) for real-time processing of LiDAR point clouds (2D plane).
  - **Actuation FE**: Low-resource nodes (e.g., 4-core CPU, 4GB RAM) for real-time servo/motor control.
- **Storage**:
  - **Data Storage FE**: Temporarily caches raw LiDAR point clouds (e.g., 10GB+ for 1-hour continuous data) and **persistent storage** for vehicle state reports (e.g., 1TB for 30 days).
  - **Reporting FE**: Cloud-based storage for aggregated reports (e.g., SQL databases for structured logs).

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **ROS2’s modular architecture** allows horizontal scaling of FE components (e.g., adding more LiDAR nodes) via **ROS2’s multi-master replication**.
- **Wi-Fi UDP** enables **horizontal scaling** of inter-entity communication by distributing data across multiple access points.
- **Cloud-based ACC Controlling FE** can scale via **containerization** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) for dynamic workloads.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **Onboard Deployment**: Embedded in the vehicle (e.g., LiDAR, ROS2 nodes) for real-time control.
- **Edge Deployment**: Lightweight FE components (e.g., Actuation FE) deployed on edge devices (e.g., Raspberry Pi).
- **Cloud Deployment**: Heavy processing (e.g., ACC Controlling FE, Reporting FE) offloaded to cloud infrastructure (e.g., AWS/GCP).

**Availability & Reliability Assurance**:
- **ROS2’s built-in reliability** ensures **99.9% uptime** for intra-Digital Twin communication via **message timeouts** and **retry policies**.
- **Wi-Fi UDP** assumes **99.5% availability** with redundant access points for critical data streams.
- **No explicit SLA or redundancy mechanisms** are detailed, but the system assumes **failover protocols** (e.g., automatic switch to backup Wi-Fi) for reliability.
"
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must incorporate **data governance frameworks** aligned with **ISO 26262-1:2018** for functional safety in road vehicles, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations implicitly through adherence to safety-critical standards. While explicit data ownership policies are not detailed in the provided documents, the system should enforce **consent management** for data sharing in **v2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** as per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, which mandates secure communication protocols (e.g., **EPS, ETSI ITS-G5**) for vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) and vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) interactions, requiring explicit user consent for data collection and processing.

For **data anonymization**, the system should leverage **ISO 15622**’s emphasis on adaptive cruise control (ACC) performance testing, where real-time sensor data (e.g., LiDAR, radar) must be processed with **differential privacy techniques** to prevent re-identification in shared datasets. **Data sharing agreements** would be governed by **3GPP’s security frameworks**, ensuring compliance with **GDPR-like principles** through **end-to-end encryption (E2EE)** and **zero-trust architectures** for V2X communications. No specific tools or methodologies for anonymization are mentioned, but **ISO 26262’s risk-based approach** (e.g., SIL levels) dictates that privacy-preserving measures must be **safety-critical** and auditable.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard is the **primary compliance framework** for Digital Twins in manufacturing, structured into four parts with **Part 2** defining the **entity-based reference architecture (RA)**. Key standardization aspects include:

1. **Industry Standards and Specifications**:
   - **ISO 23247-2**: Reference architecture with **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**.
   - **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**: Adopted for **data exchange** and **interoperability** between FEs and OMEs (e.g., real-time data streams).

2. **Certification and Compliance**:
   - The standard does not specify certification requirements but implies adherence to **ISO 23247-1 (general principles)** and **ISO 23247-3 (digital representation)** for validation.

3. **Interoperability Standards**:
   - **OPC UA** is the explicit interoperability protocol for DT data exchange, ensuring compatibility across entities (e.g., FE-to-OME communication).

4. **Public Demonstration Compliance**:
   - The publicly available DT demonstration ([2]) likely follows ISO 23247 principles but does not provide details on compliance verification.

*Note*: The documents emphasize ISO 23247’s foundational role but do not elaborate on certification bodies, compliance frameworks, or additional standards beyond OPC UA.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must adhere to **ISO 26262-1:2018** for **functional safety**, implementing **ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) assessments** to classify system components (e.g., ACC, autonomous driving modules) and enforce **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., redundant sensors, failover protocols). **Cybersecurity protocols** for V2X services (per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**) include:
- **Authentication**: Use of **X.509 certificates** and **EAP-TLS** for secure peer verification in V2I/V2V communications.
- **Access control**: Role-based access (RBAC) via **ITS-G5** or **EPS** standards, restricting data access to authorized entities (e.g., traffic management centers).
- **Threat mitigation**: **ISO 26262’s risk-based approach** requires **real-time intrusion detection** (e.g., anomaly detection in ACC control loops) and **hardware root-of-trust** (e.g., **TPM 2.0** modules) for cryptographic operations.
- **Fail-safe mechanisms**: **ISO 15622** specifies **performance test procedures** for ACC, including **fail-safe modes** (e.g., emergency braking triggers) activated if digital twin discrepancies exceed thresholds (e.g., ±5% deviation in speed control).
- **Risk assessment**: **3GPP’s threat modeling** (e.g., **OWASP Top 10 for IoT**) must be integrated, with **penetration testing** (e.g., **automated fuzzing tools**) for V2X interfaces.
No explicit mention of **model-driven architectures** (per Dalibor et al., 2020) for interactive cockpits is directly tied to security, but **ISO 26262’s safety-criticality** implies that **digital twin validation** (e.g., simulation-to-reality alignment) must be auditable and tamper-proof via **blockchain-based logs** (not explicitly named but implied for traceability).
"
]
